title: Israeli officials offer their condolences after death of US Senator Lindsey Graham
text:
Israeli officials offer their condolences after death of US Senator Lindsey Graham

Jul 12, 2026

Add AL-MONITOR on Google

FILE PHOTO: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, U.S. Republican Senator Lindsey Graham and U.S. Ambassador to Israel David Friedman visit the border line between Israel and Syria at the Israeli-occupied Golan Heights March 11, 2019. REUTERS/Ronen Zvulun/File Photo — Ronen Zvulun

July 12 (Reuters) - Israel's prime minister and other leading officials offered their condolences on Sunday for the death of U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham, saying they had lost a dear friend."Lindsey understood that the security of Israel and America are inseparable," Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said in a statement."Israel has lost one of its greatest friends. America has lost a great patriot. I have lost a beloved friend."In separate posts on X, Israeli President Isaac Herzog, Defense Minister Israel Katz, Foreign Minister Gideon Saar and former prime minister Naftali Bennett called Graham a true friend of Israel and one of its strongest supporters.(Reporting by Tala Ramadan, Nayera Abdallah and Maayan LubellEditing by David Goodman)
